brief_nursing_elaboration: Thyroid hormone excess from Graves/toxic nodule. Nursing: beta-blockers, antithyroid meds, iodine solution, symptom management, weight monitoring, exophthalmos care if Graves.
Pyloric muscle hypertrophy obstructing gastric outlet. Nursing: pre-op hydration/electrolyte repletion, post-pyloromyotomy feeding progression, vomiting precautions, metabolic alkalosis management.
Pancreatic tumor producing excess somatostatin. Nursing: steatorrhea management, diarrhea assessment, nutritional supplementation, diagnostic octreotide scan coordination.
Excessive endogenous/exogenous insulin. Nursing: blood glucose monitoring, carbohydrate intake, insulin adjustment, hypoglycemia precautions, education on recognition.
Adrenal/extra-adrenal catecholamine tumor. Nursing: BP monitoring, phentolamine admin, beta-blocker titration, urinary metanephrine monitoring, surgical preparation.
Severe hyperthyroidism/thyroid storm. Nursing: beta-blockers, PTU/methimazole admin, iodine solution, fever management, cardiac monitoring, anxiolytic support.
Thyroid hormone deficiency from iodine/autoimmune. Nursing: levothyroxine admin (empty stomach), TSH monitoring, energy assessment, weight management, cold intolerance education.
Leukemia/lymphoma/myeloma spectrum. Nursing: chemotherapy coordination, CBC monitoring, infection precautions, nutritional support, psychosocial support, symptom management.

literal_translation: Multiple ovarian cysts with hormonal dysfunction
brief_nursing_elaboration: PCOS with hyperandrogenism/insulin resistance. Nursing: metformin admin, oral contraceptives, weight reduction, fertility counseling, metabolic syndrome screening.
Type 2 diabetes from insulin resistance. Nursing: oral antidiabetic agents, diet/exercise modification, glucose monitoring, cardiovascular risk reduction, complication screening.
Type 1 diabetes from autoimmune beta-cell destruction. Nursing: insulin therapy (multiple injection/pump), glucose monitoring, carbohydrate counting, hypoglycemia/DKA management.

prefix_meaning: Greek: ketone = product of fat metabolism
brief_nursing_elaboration: DKA from insulin deficiency. Nursing: insulin drip, fluid resuscitation, electrolyte replacement, glucose/ketone monitoring, HCO3 correction, ICU monitoring.

root_2_meaning: Greek: glykys = sweet + emia = blood condition
literal_translation: High blood glucose with elevated serum osmolality
brief_nursing_elaboration: HHS from severe hyperglycemia. Nursing: fluid resuscitation (hypotonic), insulin drip, electrolyte monitoring, glucose/osmolality trending, ICU care, underlying cause identification.

root_2_meaning: Greek: glykys = sweet + emia = blood condition
literal_translation: Inability to recognize low blood glucose
brief_nursing_elaboration: Loss of hypoglycemic warning signs from recurrent episodes. Nursing: frequent glucose monitoring, continuous glucose monitor, driver safety assessment, insulin pump consideration.
Type I-IV hypersensitivity (anaphylaxis/GVHD/serum sickness). Nursing: allergen avoidance, epinephrine/antihistamines ready, desensitization consideration, reaction monitoring.
T-cell deficiency (DiGeorge/SCID/HIV). Nursing: infection prevention, PCP/fungal prophylaxis, immunization assessment, transplant consideration for SCID, antiretroviral therapy for HIV.
B-cell/antibody deficiency (X-linked agammaglobulinemia). Nursing: IVIG replacement, infection prevention, live vaccine avoidance, antibiotic prophylaxis, immunization education.
Sepsis with hypotension/organ dysfunction. Nursing: broad-spectrum antibiotics, fluid resuscitation, vasopressor support, lactate monitoring, source control, ICU care.

prefix_meaning: Greek: haima = blood + rhegnynai = to burst
brief_nursing_elaboration: Shock from acute hemorrhage. Nursing: IV access, crystalloid/blood product infusion, hemoglobin monitoring, bleeding source identification/control, trauma activation.

prefix_meaning: Greek: kardia = heart + gennao = to produce
literal_translation: Circulatory collapse from cardiac pump failure
brief_nursing_elaboration: Shock from MI/arrhythmia/valve rupture. Nursing: inotropes, vasopressors, mechanical support (IABP/ECMO), coronary intervention, cardiac output assessment.
Anaphylaxis from allergen exposure. Nursing: epinephrine IM immediately, IV access, antihistamines, corticosteroids, airway management, observation for biphasic reaction.

root_2_meaning: Latin: volumen = volume + emia = blood condition
literal_translation: Circulatory collapse from decreased blood volume
brief_nursing_elaboration: Shock from volume loss (hemorrhage/dehydration). Nursing: IV access, rapid fluid resuscitation, bleeding control, hemodynamic monitoring, underlying cause treatment.

literal_translation: Circulatory collapse from maldistribution of blood
brief_nursing_elaboration: Septic/anaphylactic/spinal shock. Nursing: antibiotics/epinephrine based on cause, fluid resuscitation, vasopressors, underlying pathology treatment.
Tension pneumothorax/PE/tamponade shock. Nursing: needle decompression/chest tube, thrombolytic/anticoagulant, pericardiocentesis, underlying cause treatment.
Alkalemia from hyperventilation (anxiety/pain/hypoxia). Nursing: address underlying cause, controlled breathing, pain management, CO2 retention monitoring.
Acidemia from hypoventilation (COPD/apnea/sedation). Nursing: airway management, mechanical ventilation, CO2 retention treatment, underlying cause identification.

literal_translation: Elevated pH from HCO3 excess/H+ loss
brief_nursing_elaboration: Alkalemia from loss of H+ (vomiting/diuretics) or HCO3 gain. Nursing: saline administration, address underlying cause, potassium repletion, hypokalemia correction.

literal_translation: Decreased pH from HCO3 loss/acid gain
brief_nursing_elaboration: Acidemia from lactic/ketoacidosis or HCO3 loss. Nursing: address underlying cause, sodium bicarbonate if severe, hyperkalemia management, renal/hepatic function assessment.
Two or more concurrent acid-base abnormalities. Nursing: identify each component, treat underlying causes (sepsis/toxin/renal), ABG trending, ICU monitoring.
Life-threatening hypercalcemia (>14 mg/dL). Nursing: aggressive hydration, loop diuretics, bisphosphonates, calcitonin, hemodialysis if refractory, cardiac monitoring.

root_2_meaning: Greek: kalium = potassium + emia = blood condition
literal_translation: Muscle paralysis from low serum potassium
brief_nursing_elaboration: Paralysis from severe hypokalemia. Nursing: potassium replacement IV (cautious rate), ECG monitoring, cardiac dysrhythmia precautions, underlying cause treatment (diuretics/diarrhea).
Excessive urinary calcium from hyperparathyroidism/vitamin D excess. Nursing: hydration encouragement, dietary calcium/sodium restriction, thiazide diuretic consideration, stone prevention.
Elevated serum cholesterol (total >240 mg/dL). Nursing: dietary modification, statin therapy, lipid panel monitoring, cardiovascular risk reduction, lifestyle education.
Elevated serum triglycerides (>200 mg/dL). Nursing: dietary fat/carbohydrate restriction, fibrate/statin therapy, alcohol limitation, exercise encouragement, pancreatitis precautions if severe.
Elevated cholesterol/triglyceride-carrying particles. Nursing: lipid panel interpretation by type, statin/fibrate therapy, lifestyle modification, xanthoma assessment if familial.
Autosomal dominant LDL receptor deficiency. Nursing: early statin therapy, intensive lipid lowering, premature CAD screening, cascade genetic testing, family education.
Autoimmune destruction of bile ducts. Nursing: UDCA admin, immunosuppressive meds, pruritus management, fat-soluble vitamin supplementation, liver transplant evaluation.
Bile duct fibrosis from autoimmune. Nursing: UDCA admin, cholangitis management, IBD screening/treatment, cholangiocarcinoma surveillance, liver transplant consideration.
Autoimmune liver inflammation. Nursing: corticosteroid admin, azathioprine/mycophenolate, LFT monitoring, immunosuppressive therapy coordination, cirrhosis assessment.
Cirrhosis from prolonged alcohol use. Nursing: alcohol cessation support, ascites management, variceal bleeding prevention, encephalopathy management, transplant evaluation.

literal_translation: Fat accumulation in liver without alcohol
brief_nursing_elaboration: Hepatic steatosis from metabolic syndrome. Nursing: weight loss encouragement, diabetes/lipid control, vitamin E consideration, lifestyle modification, cirrhosis precautions.

literal_translation: Anemia from accelerated red blood cell breakdown
brief_nursing_elaboration: RBC destruction from antibodies/mechanical/infection. Nursing: transfusion if severe, corticosteroids, splenectomy consideration, reticulocyte count monitoring, direct Coombs test.

literal_translation: Anemia from sickle-shaped red blood cells
brief_nursing_elaboration: Autosomal recessive hemoglobinopathy. Nursing: pain crisis management, hydroxyurea, folic acid, hydration, transfusion, genetic counseling, organ dysfunction screening.
Severe beta-thalassemia needing transfusions. Nursing: chronic transfusion management, iron chelation, folic acid, splenectomy consideration, bone marrow transplant/gene therapy.
